scenario: A Quanta Logistics operator follows the Camunda 8.8 getting-started guide on a Linux box for 8 instances. After running zbctl to create an order-process instance the operator wants to complete the Initiate Payment service task with a community zbctl worker rather than wiring up a custom Java handler from scratch for the demo.
question: Which Linux zbctl invocation does the guide use to complete the Initiate Payment service task on the instance?

options.text: The guide runs the Linux invocation ./bin/zbctl --insecure create worker initiate-payment --handler cat, which starts a community zbctl worker that completes Initiate Payment jobs using cat as the job handler
The guide runs ./bin/zbctl --insecure complete job initiate-payment, which directly marks any pending Initiate Payment job as completed without starting a long-running zbctl worker process on the box
The guide runs ./bin/zbctl --insecure assign task initiate-payment to a Tasklist user, who then completes the Initiate Payment service task interactively from the Tasklist UI in the browser

The guide runs ./bin/zbctl --insecure deploy worker initiate-payment.bpmn, which expects a worker BPMN model on disk that the engine compiles into an Initiate Payment completion routine on the instance

a.text: Correct. The guide shows the Linux invocation ./bin/zbctl --insecure create worker initiate-payment --handler cat, which starts a community zbctl worker that completes the Initiate Payment service task using cat as the handler.

b.text: Incorrect. The guide drives the Initiate Payment task with a zbctl worker rather than a zbctl complete job command, so the documented flow is not a direct one-shot completion of the pending job.

c.text: Incorrect. The guide does not assign the task to a Tasklist user; the Initiate Payment service task is completed by a zbctl worker process that uses a shell command as its handler.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no documented zbctl deploy worker command for an initiate-payment.bpmn model; the guide starts a worker process directly with create worker --handler cat on Linux.

explanation: On Linux the guide runs ./bin/zbctl --insecure create worker initiate-payment --handler cat to start a community zbctl worker that completes the Initiate Payment service task. The shell cat command serves as the job handler in the demo.
